commands.review_summary:
The PR gates standalone and hosted GitHub issue-comment fast ack on parser-compatible commands, adds inline-mention regression coverage, and makes a few workflow-file test reads CRLF-tolerant.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main's fast-ack regex can match the reported inline mention while parseCommand only accepts command-shaped lines. I did not run a live webhook server in this read-only review.

target.title: Require routable ClawSweeper commands before fast ack
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes openclaw/clawsweeper#232.

This makes the fast-ack paths reject comments that only mention `@clawsweeper` inline inside prose, unless the same comment is actually routable by the command parser.

Changes:

- gates the deterministic comment webhook on `parseCommand(...)` before posting the visible fast ack
- replaces the hosted dashboard worker's loose mention regex with a parser-compatible command-line classifier
- keeps valid command forms working, including an own-line mention and the `@clawsweeper` then next-line `re-review` pattern used by issue authors
- adds regression coverage for inline mentions versus real command-line mentions
- normalizes a few workflow test reads so CRLF workflow files do not break unrelated assertions

## Testing

- `pnpm run build:all`
- `pnpm run lint`
- `node --test test\\repair\\comment-router-core.test.ts test\\repair\\comment-webhook.test.ts test\\dashboard-worker.test.ts`
- `node --test --test-name-pattern "sweep workflow_dispatch input count|sweep review continuations|repair workflows preserve" test\\clawsweeper.test.ts`

Note: I also tried `pnpm run check` locally on Windows. The relevant build/lint/regression parts pass, but the full suite still hits unrelated Windows/local-environment failures around spawning `codex` and POSIX file-mode assertions.
target.author: brokemac79
